metadata.title: States PCS: Karnataka GK for KPSC and other Karnataka State Exams

questions.question: Harihara I and Bukka Raya I, who founded the Sangama dynasty, were originally feudatories of __?
questions.explanation: Harihara I and Bukka Raya I founded the Sangama dynasty of the Vijayanagara Empire in 1336. They served as feudatories under the Kakatiya rulers of Warangal before its fall to Muslim invaders in 1323, after which they fled to Anegondi. Alternative accounts state they served the Hoysala king Veera Ballala III.

questions.question: Which of the following tried to promote education of his Muslim subjects by establishing schools where students were given free lodging facilities?
questions.explanation: Muhammad Shah II tried to promote education of his Muslim subjects. He established schools where students were given free lodging facilities. He regarded himself as the trustee of the resources of his kingdom and discouraged all useless expenditure.

questions.question: Which of the following kings defeated the Gond Raja Narsing Rai?
questions.explanation: From 1397 to 1422 CE, Taj-ud-din Firoz Shah ruled the Bahmani kingdom, expanding into Berar by defeating Gond Raja Narsing Rai. He was a patron of arts, a polyglot, and established a university in Bidar. Following the defeat, Narsing Rai paid a tribute and married his daughter to Firoz Shah, strengthening alliances.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about Bahmani architecture: The tomb of Alauddin I built in the fourteenth century is an imitation of the ‘ Tughlaq style of the Delhi Sultanate. The tombs built by the Bahmani rulers in the fifteenth century at Ashtur in Bidar depict distinct Persian elements neatly combined with those of the northern Indian style. Select the correct option from the codes given below:
questions.explanation: The tomb of Alauddin I which was built in the fourteenth century is an imitation of the ‘ Tughlaq style of the Delhi Sultanate. The tombs built by the Bahmani rulers in the fifteenth century at Ashtur in Bidar showcase distinct Persian elements neatly combined with those of the northern Indian style.

questions.question: Which of the following pairs is correctly matched regarding Bahmani military terms? Barq-andaz – Matchlock-men/Musketeers Golandaz – Artillerymen/Gunners Paigah – Naval commanders Select the correct option from the codes given below:
questions.explanation: Barq-andaz (literally “lightning-throwers”) refers to infantry armed with muskets, and Golandaz (literally “ball-throwers”) were artillerymen. These correspond to Statements 1 and 2. Paigah were elite cavalry or household troops, not naval commanders, making Statement 3 incorrect.

questions.question: Which of the following succeeded Muhammad Qutb Shah?
questions.explanation: Abdullah Qutb Shah was the sixth ruler and succeeded Muhammad Qutb Shah. He enjoyed a long reign of 46 years. He also made further additions to the Golconda fort which make it impregnable.

questions.question: During the time of western ganga dynasty who was known by the name of sandhivirgrahi?
questions.explanation: During the time of western ganga dynasty foreign minister was known by the name of sandhivirgrahi.

questions.question: The stone inscription found at Halmidi belongs to which of the following Kadamba rulers?
questions.explanation: The stone inscription found at Halmidi has been assigned to C. 450 CE. and belongs to Kadamba ruler Kakusthavarma, whose reign is estimated to be between 435 CE to 455 CE.

questions.question: Who of the following gave the account regarding presence of slavery in the Vijayanagar Empire?
questions.explanation: Nicolo de Conti, an Italian merchant, documented slavery in the Vijayanagar Empire, a wealthy cultural hub. Slaves were used for domestic work and agriculture, and markets traded spices, textiles, and humans. Slavery was integral to the empire’s economy and social hierarchy. Conti’s writings offer insight into medieval South India’s socio-economic structure.

questions.question: The capital of Hoysala Empire was moved from Belur to:
questions.explanation: The capital of the empire initially resided at Belur, later moving to Halebidu.
